entries.description: Read project-guidance.md, lessons-learned.md, ai-led-session-workflow.md, spec.md; reviewed prior session reviews from set 001; user instructed router restricted to end-of-session verification only this session and to use Gemini Pro as the verifier.
Wrote session-state.json marking session 1 of set 002 in-progress (currentSession=1, lifecycleState=work_in_progress, orchestrator=claude-code/claude-opus-4-7@high, totalSessions=4).
Per durable user instruction (memory: feedback_ai_router_usage), routed ai-assignment.md authoring intentionally skipped; sessions 1-5 of set 001 also skipped this for consistency.

Created ai-router/verifier_role.py: VerifierDaemon class with run_one() and run_forever(); make_worker_id() builds <hostname>:<pid>:<provider>:<8-hex-rand>; FollowUpRequested exception; process_one_message() owns the per-message lifecycle (heartbeat thread + claim->verifier->complete/follow-up/fail); _HeartbeatThread is a daemon thread that beats every interval, exits silently on ConcurrencyError (lease loss) by setting lost_lease=True; reclaim_expired() runs opportunistically before each claim so recovery proceeds without an external scheduler; SIGTERM/SIGINT install_signal_handlers wires graceful shutdown via a threading.Event; in-flight job is allowed to finish before exit; argparse CLI with --provider/--base-dir/--poll-interval/--lease-seconds/--heartbeat-interval; restart-safe: never steals a prior worker_ids active claim (relies on lease+reclaim).
Re-exported FollowUpRequested, HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_SECONDS, DEFAULT_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS, VerifierDaemon, make_worker_id, process_one_message, run_verification from ai-router/__init__.py.

Created ai-router/tests/test_verifier_role.py with 23 unit + integration tests across 9 classes: worker-id shape (format/uniqueness/provider-in-id), happy path (returns completed and writes result, payload passed unchanged), follow-up round-trip (persists question and leaves message claimed; max-rounds-exceeded propagation), failure (transitions to new with retries, transitions to failed when exhausted), heartbeat (extends lease during long work, thread exits silently on lease loss), VerifierDaemon.run_one (none on empty, processes pending, calls reclaim before claim), run_forever shutdown (idle loop exits promptly, in-flight job completes before shutdown), restart safety (does not steal prior worker active claim), CLI argparser (provider required, defaults, overrides), real subprocess CLI integration (driver-script monkey-patches run_verification, daemon polls real SQLite DB, observes completion within 10s, terminate cleanup).

End-of-session verification routed via gemini-pro per user instruction (override of router-config default gpt-5-4 for this session). Verdict: VERIFIED on round 1. Zero issues. All four Session 1 acceptance criteria explicitly affirmed.
Per durable user instruction (memory: feedback_ai_router_usage), routed next-orchestrator recommendation skipped - router restricted to end-of-session verification only this session.

Read project-guidance.md, lessons-learned.md, session-set-authoring-guide.md, ai-led-session-workflow.md, spec.md (Session 2 plan), session-state.json, activity-log.json, session-1 review; verifier_role.py, queue_db.py, ai-router/__init__.py for the orchestrator-role design baseline. User instruction: ai-router restricted to end-of-session verification only this session, route verification to gemini-pro.
Per durable user instruction (memory: feedback_ai_router_usage), skipped routed authoring of ai-assignment.md; same as set 001 sessions and set 002 session 1.

Created ai-router/orchestrator_role.py: OrchestratorDaemon class mirroring VerifierDaemon (same lease/heartbeat/shutdown/reclaim plumbing reused via process_one_message and make_worker_id from verifier_role); TASK_VERIFICATION_FOLLOWUP=verification_followup and TASK_VERIFICATION_REJECTED=verification_rejected constants (ORCHESTRATOR_TASK_TYPES tuple); make_dispatch_verifier(followup_handler, rejection_handler) builds the callable passed to process_one_message and routes by msg.task_type, raising UnknownTaskTypeError on unrecognized types so the queue fail/retry/timeout machinery applies; pluggable handlers (defaults raise NotImplementedError because Session 3 wires the production router-backed handlers); FollowUpRequested re-exported on this module so callers wiring production handlers do not need a verifier_role import; argparse CLI with --provider/--base-dir/--poll-interval/--lease-seconds/--heartbeat-interval; documented that this daemon does NOT generate session work autonomously - it only handles asynchronous follow-up/rejection traffic between human-typed trigger phrases.
Re-exported ORCHESTRATOR_TASK_TYPES, OrchestratorDaemon, TASK_VERIFICATION_FOLLOWUP, TASK_VERIFICATION_REJECTED, UnknownTaskTypeError, make_dispatch_verifier from ai-router/__init__.py.

Created ai-router/tests/test_orchestrator_role.py with 22 unit + integration tests across 11 classes: task-type constants (canonical strings + tuple covers both); dispatch routing (followup -> followup_handler, rejected -> rejection_handler, unknown task_type raises UnknownTaskTypeError, FollowUpRequested propagates through dispatch); run_one followup (reply-and-complete via dict return, continue-dialogue via FollowUpRequested - left claimed, follow-up persisted under claude provider); run_one rejection (acknowledged-and-completed with payload + from_provider captured); run_one failures (UnknownTaskTypeError routes through queue fail path with class name in failure_reason; handler RuntimeError transitions to new with attempts=1); default handlers are stubs (both default handlers raise NotImplementedError caught by process_one_message and message ends up failed); run_one ordering (reclaim before claim; None on empty queue); graceful shutdown (idle loop exits within 2s; in-flight job completes before shutdown returns); CLI argparser (provider required, defaults, overrides); multi-round dialogue integration (full verifier->followup->orchestrator-reply->verifier-completion round-trip with both daemons + lease-expire-then-reclaim cycle); both daemons coexist as real subprocesses (each with stubbed handler in driver script, message-completion polled until both done within 15s).

End-of-session verification routed via gemini-pro per user instruction (override of router-config default for this session). Verdict: VERIFIED (verifier wrote "implementation meets all deliverables and acceptance criteria"). One minor code-clarity suggestion: replace magic number 2 with signal.SIGINT in subprocess integration test - applied; tests still pass (22/22).
Applied the verifier minor suggestion: imported signal and replaced proc.send_signal(2) with signal.SIGINT in TestBothDaemonsCoexistAsSubprocesses. Re-ran orchestrator_role tests: 22/22 passed.
Per durable user instruction (memory: feedback_ai_router_usage), skipped routed next-orchestrator recommendation; ai-router restricted to end-of-session verification only this session.
